levels.description: Has not yet built or maintained REST endpoints in a production backend.
Builds simple CRUD endpoints, implement request validation, and return standard HTTP responses.
Designs resource-based APIs, implement versioning and error handling, and integrate authentication into services.
Designs scalable API contracts, optimize endpoint performance, and govern consistency across multiple backend services.
Has not yet designed database schemas or modeled data for backend applications.
Creates simple tables, define relationships, and write basic queries for application features.
Designs normalized schemas, implement indexes and constraints, and evolve data models safely through migrations.
Designs complex data architectures, optimize query patterns at scale, and guide database strategy for reliability and growth.
Has not yet implemented repository logic or worked with an ORM in production code.
Implements basic entity mapping, build simple queries, and perform standard create, read, update, and delete operations.
Designs efficient data access layers, implement transaction handling, and reduce common query inefficiencies.
Optimizes complex persistence flows, design resilient data access patterns, and balance ORM use with direct SQL where needed.
Has not yet implemented login, token handling, or access control in backend systems.
Implements basic authentication flows, validate tokens, and enforce simple role-based permissions.
Designs secure access control rules, implement refresh and revocation flows, and integrate external identity providers.
Designs enterprise-grade security models, optimize authorization across services, and lead remediation of access control risks.
Has not yet implemented application or distributed caching for backend performance.
Implements simple in-memory or distributed caching, store common lookups, and invalidate entries with basic rules.
Designs cache strategies, implement expiration and invalidation patterns, and reduce database load for high-traffic features.
Optimizes multi-layer caching architectures, design cache consistency approaches, and tune performance for large-scale systems.
Has not yet built asynchronous workflows using queues or event-driven processing.
Implements basic producers and consumers, publish simple messages, and handle straightforward background tasks.
Designs reliable queue workflows, implement retry and dead-letter handling, and monitor message processing behavior.
Designs scalable event-driven systems, optimize throughput and failure recovery, and govern messaging standards across services.
Has not yet implemented scheduled or long-running background jobs in backend applications.
Builds simple scheduled jobs, implement basic retry behavior, and process routine tasks outside user requests.
Designs resilient job workflows, implement idempotent processing, and handle execution failures without data corruption.
Optimizes distributed job execution, design recovery strategies for large workloads, and lead background processing architecture decisions.
Has not yet integrated backend services with external APIs or third-party platforms.
Implements basic API clients, handle authentication with external services, and map responses into application models.
Designs reliable integration flows, implement timeout and retry logic, and manage contract changes with partner systems.
Designs robust integration architecture, optimize resilience across multiple dependencies, and lead strategies for interoperability and failure isolation.
Has not yet built or maintained backend features in a microservices environment.
Builds features within existing services, implement service-to-service calls, and follow established service boundaries.
Designs service responsibilities, implement inter-service communication patterns, and handle data consistency across boundaries.
Designs scalable service ecosystems, optimize observability and fault isolation, and guide decomposition of complex backend domains.
Has not yet analyzed or improved backend performance bottlenecks in production workloads.
Measures basic response times, identify slow queries or endpoints, and implement straightforward optimizations.
Designs targeted improvements using profiling data, optimize I/O and database usage, and reduce latency under typical load.
Leads performance tuning across services, design scalable throughput strategies, and optimize systems for sustained high-volume traffic.
Has not yet implemented structured logging, metrics, or runtime monitoring in backend systems.
Implements application logs, capture key errors and events, and use dashboards to inspect service health.
Designs useful observability signals, implement metrics and traces, and investigate incidents using correlated telemetry.
Designs end-to-end observability standards, optimize alert quality, and lead monitoring strategy for complex distributed systems.
Has not yet built automated tests for backend logic, APIs, or data access.
Builds unit tests for core functions, implement basic API tests, and verify expected behavior for simple scenarios.
Designs maintainable test suites, implement integration tests with realistic dependencies, and prevent regressions in critical flows.
Leads backend test strategy, optimize reliability of automated validation, and enforce quality gates across delivery pipelines.
Has not yet configured automated build, test, or deployment pipelines for backend applications.
Implements basic pipeline steps to build services, run tests, and deploy to non-production environments.
Designs reliable delivery workflows, implement environment-specific deployment rules, and automate rollback or approval steps.
Designs scalable release pipelines, optimize deployment safety and speed, and govern delivery standards across backend teams.
Has not yet packaged or deployed backend services using containers or orchestration platforms.
Builds container images, implement basic runtime configuration, and deploy services using existing infrastructure patterns.
Designs deployment manifests, implement health checks and scaling settings, and troubleshoot container runtime issues.
Designs production-grade container platforms, optimize deployment resilience, and lead rollout strategies for distributed services.
Has not yet applied secure coding practices to protect backend services and data.
Implements input validation, handle secrets safely, and prevent common issues such as injection and insecure exposure.
Designs secure service patterns, implement dependency and vulnerability controls, and remediate identified backend risks.
Leads secure backend design reviews, optimize defensive controls, and drive remediation strategy for high-impact security threats.
Has not yet designed backend code to handle concurrency, contention, or workload scaling.
Builds services that handle simple parallel requests, implement safe shared resource access, and avoid obvious blocking issues.
Designs concurrent processing flows, implement throttling and synchronization patterns, and improve stability under growing load.
Designs highly scalable backend systems, optimize resource utilization under concurrency, and lead architecture decisions for sustained growth.
Has not yet built production user interfaces using semantic HTML structure.
Builds simple page layouts, use correct semantic elements, and structure forms and content for basic usability.
Designs maintainable markup structures, implement accessible document patterns, and support reusable component-based layouts.
Defines robust markup standards, optimize semantic consistency across large applications, and guide implementation for complex UI systems.
Has not yet implemented production styling for web interfaces.
Builds basic component styles, implement layouts with modern CSS, and apply consistent spacing and typography.
Designs scalable styling patterns, implement theme-aware components, and organize styles to reduce duplication and conflicts.
Defines styling architecture for large frontends, optimize maintainability across design systems, and lead adoption of consistent visual standards.
Has not yet built interactive frontend features using JavaScript or TypeScript.
Builds simple interactive behavior, implement typed models for basic components, and handle common browser events.
Designs maintainable client-side logic, implement reusable typed utilities, and manage asynchronous UI behavior safely.
Defines frontend coding standards, optimize complex stateful logic, and lead TypeScript design for large-scale applications.
Has not yet built production interfaces with a modern frontend framework.
Builds simple components, implement props and local state, and connect views to existing application flows.
Designs reusable component structures, implement predictable rendering patterns, and manage feature-level composition cleanly.
Defines frontend architecture with framework best practices, optimize rendering behavior, and guide reusable patterns across teams.
Has not yet implemented shared or complex client-side state in production applications.
Implements local and shared state for simple screens, update UI from user actions, and handle basic data flows.
Designs maintainable state models, implement predictable updates across components, and coordinate async data with UI state.
Defines scalable state management strategies, optimize reactivity for complex applications, and guide patterns for long-term maintainability.
Has not yet built interfaces that adapt reliably across screen sizes and devices.
Builds layouts that adjust for common desktop and mobile sizes, implement basic breakpoints, and fix obvious overflow issues.
Designs responsive components, implement adaptive layout behavior, and ensure consistent usability across varied viewports.
Defines responsive design standards, optimize complex multi-device experiences, and lead implementation for high-traffic frontend products.
Has not yet implemented accessibility requirements in production frontend work.
Builds accessible forms and navigation, implement labels and keyboard support, and address basic semantic issues.
Designs interfaces that meet common accessibility standards, implement screen reader-friendly patterns, and test key interaction paths.
Defines accessibility practices across frontend systems, optimize inclusive interaction design, and lead remediation of complex compliance gaps.
Has not yet connected frontend applications to backend APIs in production.
Implements simple data fetching, render API results in UI components, and handle basic loading and error states.
Designs reliable client data flows, implement caching or refetch behavior, and coordinate API state with user interactions.
Defines frontend integration patterns, optimize data loading for complex applications, and lead strategies for resilient API consumption.
Has not yet built automated tests for frontend components or user flows.
Builds unit tests for simple components, implement assertions for UI behavior, and verify common rendering scenarios.
Designs maintainable test coverage, implement integration or end-to-end tests, and prevent regressions in critical user journeys.
Defines frontend test strategy, optimize reliability of automated validation, and lead quality practices across large UI codebases.
Has not yet measured or improved rendering and loading performance in frontend applications.
Identifies simple performance issues, implement basic asset or rendering improvements, and reduce obvious page load delays.
Designs targeted optimizations using runtime metrics, implement code splitting and efficient rendering patterns, and improve interaction speed.
Defines frontend performance strategy, optimize large-scale applications for speed and stability, and lead remediation of complex bottlenecks.
Has not yet configured frontend build pipelines or bundling tools.
Runs and adjust existing build configurations, implement basic environment settings, and troubleshoot common bundling issues.
Designs build workflows for multiple environments, implement asset optimization steps, and improve development and deployment efficiency.
Defines scalable frontend build architecture, optimize bundling and delivery performance, and guide tooling standards across projects.
Has not yet designed reusable UI components for production frontend systems.
Builds simple reusable components, implement consistent props and variants, and support common interface patterns.
Designs composable component APIs, implement reusable patterns across features, and reduce duplication in interface code.
Defines component system standards, optimize reuse and consistency at scale, and lead design system implementation for frontend teams.
Has not yet debugged production frontend issues using browser tools and runtime diagnostics.
Uses browser tools to inspect DOM, network requests, and console errors, and fix straightforward UI defects.
Investigates complex client-side issues, implement fixes for rendering and data flow defects, and reproduce bugs across browsers.
Leads diagnosis of difficult frontend production issues, optimize debugging workflows, and guide teams through cross-browser problem resolution.
